criteria.label: State the max-margin goal, soft-margin objective with C, and identify support vectors on a toy dataset.
criteria.example: Draw 6 separable 2D points, sketch wᵀx+b=0 and margin lines at ±1, circle support vectors, write minimize ||w||² + C Σξᵢ s.t. yᵢ(wᵀxᵢ+b) ≥ 1−ξᵢ.

criteria.label: Explain geometric vs functional margin and compute geometric margin for one labeled point.
criteria.example: Given w=(3,4), b=−5, x=(1,1), y=+1: functional margin = 2, ||w||=5, geometric margin = 2/5 = 0.4.

criteria.label: Fit a linear SVM in code, report support vector count, and predict with decision_function.
criteria.example: LinearSVC(C=1.0) on 4-point XOR-infeasible but separable-with-slack data; print len(support_), decision_function scores, and sign predictions.

criteria.label: Compare SVM vs logistic regression objective and choose which to use when calibrated probabilities are required.
criteria.example: State margin+hinge vs sigmoid+BCE; for medical risk needing P(disease), pick logistic regression or Platt-scaled SVM; for sparse text baseline with AUC, pick linear SVM.
